title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. elata
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. elata, also known as the tall marsh orchid, is a subspecies of orchid that belongs to the genus Dactylorhiza. This perennial plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. elata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, sore throats, and skin conditions.
